name: Sandy Beach
tagline: Oahu's most notorious shore break — where locals prove themselves and lifeguards stay busy.
bestTime: Best at 2–4 ft swell for bodyboarding; any day for watching from the sand
talkStory: Locals just call it Sandy's, and it holds the grim distinction of sending more people to the emergency room than any other beach in Hawaii. The powerful shore break slams onto a steep sand shelf, and even experienced bodysurfers get worked. From Here to Eternity's iconic beach scene was filmed nearby, and the grassy bluff above (Koko Crater) is Oahu's premier kite-flying and hang-gliding launch. The parking lot scene on weekends is its own spectator sport — half of east Honolulu shows up to tailgate, watch the bodyboarders, and talk story.
